title: A lack of electricity and internet access hinders AI adoption in Sub-Saharan Africa
content:

title: Friends no more: How Kyrgyzstan’s ruling tandem crumbled overnight
content:
After five years of ruling together, the president and security chief ended their political union

Originally published on Global Voices

Former security chief, Kamchybek Tashiyev (on the left) saluting president Sadyr Japarov (on the right). Photo by the Kyrgyzstan President's Office. Fair use.

title: India’s race to adopt AI sparks a deeper question: How can technology respect human rights?
content:
Technologies like AI-enabled surveillance and facial recognition are restricting civic space and aiding state authoritarianism

Originally published on Global Voices

Image by Paula Villar for APC. Used with permission.

This article is part of the series “Don’t ask AI, ask a peer,” a collaboration among Global Voices, the Association for Progressive Communications, and GenderIT.
